title: Services
text:
The Data Science team can support colleagues with:

Formulating research questions
Study design and analysis plans
Sample size and power calculations
Statistical analyses
Machine learning and prediction modelling
Routine Outcome Monitoring reports
Data cleaning and validation
Dashboards and automated reports
Manuscript methods and results sections
Reproducible R workflows

title: Services
text:
When to contact us
You can contact the Data Science team when you are planning a study, creating or preparing a dataset, preparing a manuscript, developing a report, working with clinical data, or exploring possibilities for prediction modelling.

title: Data Science @ ARQ
text:
Data Science Group
The Data Science (DS) group at ARQ Nationaal Psychotrauma Centrum provides statistical collaboration, data-analytic methodological support, and data management for psychiatric and mental health research. We collaborate with researchers across ARQ by providing data preparation, statistical expertise, reproducible analysis, and support for research projects.

title: Data Science @ ARQ
text:
Our Work
The DS group supports researchers with study design, statistical analysis, prediction modelling, data management, and manuscript preparation. We help translate complex data into reliable and actionable insights, using modern statistical and data science methods to advance the understanding, evaluation, and improvement of mental health care.

title: Data Science @ ARQ
text:
Our Aim
Our goal is to support psychiatric and mental health research through trusted biostatistical methods, innovative data-analytic approaches, and secure web-based systems for data capture, management, reporting, and statistical analysis.

title: Hassan Pazira
text:
Dr. Hassan Pazira is a statistician and data scientist at ARQ Nationaal Psychotrauma Centrum (ARQ Centrum’45), with more than a decade of experience gained through several national and international projects in advanced statistical modelling, prediction modelling, network modelling, machine learning for biomarker discovery, federated learning, programming, real-world data analysis, and reproducible statistical reporting.
He supports research and clinical projects with data preparation, methodological consultation, (high-dimensional) longitudinal and survival data analysis, mixed-effects modelling, clinical prediction modelling, Bayesian modelling, latent variable modelling, including factor analysis and latent profile/class analysis, analytics for personalized medicine, and machine learning applications in mental health care.
His methodological interests include high-dimensional statistical learning, feature and model selection, supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms, propensity score analysis, Bayesian federated learning, and methods for analysing decentralized and multi-centre data.
📧   h.pazira [at] arq.org
Google Scholar   |   LinkedIn   |   GitHub
